title: armeria maritima subsp. velutina
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. velutina, also known as Sea Thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Armeria maritima subsp. velutina include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are often used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
